questions.question: Why does a quadratic equation always have two roots?
questions.answer: Because its degree is two.

questions.question: Why does the parabola touch the x-axis when roots are equal?
questions.answer: Because the discriminant is zero.

questions.question: Why does the parabola not cut the x-axis when roots are imaginary?
questions.answer: Because there are no real solutions.

questions.question: Why is the discriminant important without solving the equation?
questions.answer: It tells the nature of roots directly.

questions.question: Why does a > 0 give a minimum value?
questions.answer: Because the parabola opens upward.

questions.question: Why does a < 0 give a maximum value?
questions.answer: Because the parabola opens downward.

questions.question: Why are algebraic methods preferred over graphical method?
questions.answer: Because they give exact solutions.

questions.question: Why does changing b shift the parabola horizontally?
questions.answer: Because b affects the axis of symmetry.

questions.question: Why does changing c shift the parabola vertically?
questions.answer: Because c is the y-intercept.

questions.question: Why does completing the square help find vertex?
questions.answer: Because it converts the equation to vertex form.

questions.question: Why do real-life optimisation problems use quadratics?
questions.answer: Because they involve maximum or minimum values.

questions.question: Why is quadratic formula called a universal method?
questions.answer: Because it works for all quadratic equations.

questions.question: Why does a quadratic equation with D < 0 have no real roots?
questions.answer: Because square root of a negative number is not real.

questions.question: Why is the vertex equidistant from the roots?
questions.answer: Because the parabola is symmetric.

questions.question: Why does x² + 1 = 0 have no real solution?
questions.answer: Because x² is always non-negative.

questions.question: Why can roots be found from x-intercepts?
questions.answer: Because roots are points where y = 0.

questions.question: Why is the axis of symmetry important?
questions.answer: Because it divides the parabola into equal halves.

questions.question: Why does the parabola represent smooth motion?
questions.answer: Because it is a continuous curve.

questions.question: Why is b² − 4ac always used in root analysis?
questions.answer: Because it determines the nature of roots.

questions.question: Why do equal roots imply tangency to x-axis?
questions.answer: Because the curve just touches the axis.

questions.question: Why does a quadratic graph have only one turning point?
questions.answer: Because its degree is two.

questions.question: Why is the vertex the lowest or highest point?
questions.answer: Because it is the turning point of the curve.

questions.question: Why are quadratic equations useful in physics?
questions.answer: Because many motions follow parabolic paths.

questions.question: Why is the discriminant zero for perfect square quadratics?
questions.answer: Because they have repeated roots.

questions.question: Why is solving quadratic equations important in mathematics?
questions.answer: Because they model many real-life and theoretical problems.
